Tested as of: 11/25/20 Game Ver: 2.0.119.430 Divinity: Original Sin (Enhanced Edition) Recipe Guide
Underline = Generic Item; Italics = Item Not Consumed on Use		 Compiled by: CultOfTheVault


Amulets
Note: Lucky Rabbit Paw Amulet & Lucky Rabbit's Paw Amulet are different items and aren't interchangable.
(Lucky) Rabbit Paws are unique in that they can be crafted into "super" versions of the normal accessories (amulet, belt, ring)

+ Item 2
= Crafted Item

Arrows
Note: There is no recipe for Fire, Freezing, or Smokescreen Arrowheads; Cursing Arrows still exist in-game but can no longer be crafted.

Belts
Note: There is no Large Magical Tusk Belt recipe; Lucky Rabbit Paw Belt & Lucky Rabbit's Paw Belt are different items and aren't interchangable. (Lucky) Rabbit Paws are unique in that they can be crafted into "super" versions of the normal accessories (amulet, belt, ring)

+ Item 2
= Crafted Item

Notes

Potions & Poisons
Note: Debuff Potions are functionally useless since they can't be applied to grenades or arrows.
Those recipes were removed from the game but the debuff potions remain.

Rings
Note: There are four types of ring kit - Regular, Stamped, Fine, and Polished. They are functionally identical and provide no greater benefits over one another; Lucky Rabbit Paw Ring & Lucky Rabbit's Paw Ring are different items and aren't interchangable.
(Lucky) Rabbit Paws are unique in that they can be crafted into "super" versions of the normal accessories (amulet, belt, ring)

Scrolls
Note: Crafted Skill Scrolls are random upon creation. The higher your Crafting skill, the more possible spells from the associated tree that become available.


Item 1
+ Item 2
= Crafted Item
Skill Recipe Book
Notes

= Blank Air Scroll

Weapons
Note: Wands have four levels of quality. The more valuable the wand base, the more powerful the wand spell will be.
Inversely, the more valuable the wand base, the weaker the base damage will be. Wand spells are not random upon creation.













Item 1
+ Item 2
= Crafted Item
Skill Recipe Book
Notes


Iron Bar
+ Anvil
= One-Handed Sword
Blacksmithing 1 He Who Smelts It I
Stats Increase with Blacksmithing Level


Large Iron Bar
+ Anvil
= Two-Handed Sword
Blacksmithing 1 He Who Smelts It III
Stats Increase with Blacksmithing Level


Steel Bar
+ Anvil
= One-Handed Axe
Blacksmithing 1 He Who Smelts It II
Stats Increase with Blacksmithing Level


Large Steel Bar
+ Anvil
= Two-Handed Axe
Blacksmithing 2 He Who Smelts It III
Stats Increase with Blacksmithing Level


Any Knife
+ Anvil
= Dagger
Blacksmithing 1 The Adventurer's Field Guide IV
Stats Increase with Blacksmithing Level

+ Branch
= Spear
